intro: Sonner exposes a free tier while Lob starts at $260/mo. Lob is positioned for transactional emails (receipts, alerts), while Sonner leans toward apps sending transactional notifications at scale. In practice, Lob — Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in Direct Mail API. Sonner — CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Toast Notifications. Lob carries medium lock-in (Direct mail API) while Sonner is low (Open source React toast). Sonner rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Lob. Listed strengths unique to Lob include transactional emails (receipts, alerts), while Sonner lists multi-channel delivery (email, sms, voice) from one api on its side. Reach for Lob when you need transactional emails (receipts, alerts); reach for Sonner when the job is automated build/test/deploy pipelines. The honest trade-off: Lob is not built for internal projects, and Sonner is not the right call for small projects with minimal communication needs, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: Lob is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Sonner is for apps sending transactional notifications at scale; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Lob if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and the $260/mo entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Sonner if apps sending transactional notifications at scale and you need a free tier to validate fit.

faq.a: Sonner has a free tier while Lob starts at $260/mo, so at the lowest end Sonner costs less. Above the free limits, per-unit rates decide the winner, and those aren't in the summary data here. Run the numbers on your expected volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lob to Sonner?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Lob is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Direct mail API), and onboarding to Sonner should be straightforward given its low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Lob or Sonner?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, Sonner rates 5/5 while Lob rates 4/5, giving Sonner the edge. That score is a rough summary; actual day-to-day DX depends on your language, deployment target, and tolerance for edge cases. Prototype both to validate.

faq.q: Is Sonner a good alternative to Lob?
faq.a: Sonner is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is apps sending transactional notifications at scale, while Lob's is transactional emails (receipts, alerts). If your job matches Lob's profile more closely, switching to Sonner is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: Sonner is explicitly not for small projects with minimal communication needs.
